content: Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

Valdr: By the gods, you there! Please, I need help! Spriggans took my friends... I barely made it out alive. Lydia: Spriggans? Ha! Should’ve brought a real hunter, not some milk-drinkers who cry over tree-fuckers. Valdr: Damn it, woman! Niels and Ari are dead in there! I don’t give a skeever’s ass about your Stormcloak bravado right now—I need aid! Lydia: Ugh, fine. But if I have to step into that nasty cave, you better pray to Dibella those twig-bitches don’t make me break a nail. MJR likes my hands smooth for when I— Valdr: Just... do you have a healing potion or not?! Lydia: Relax, twig-boy. MJR’s got plenty. But remember—if you slow us down, I’ll feed your corpse to the next bear we find. Valdr: By Ysmir, you’re insufferable. But if you help me kill those spriggans, I’ll make sure you get first crack at looting their filthy roots. Lydia: Now that’s what I like to hear. Maybe I’ll take one of their heads as a trophy. MJR gets off watching me mount creatures on my greatsword. Valdr: Divines preserve me... just follow me in, and keep your perversions to yourself till we’re done. Lydia: Oh please, like you’ve never fucked a hagraven.
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Valdr: Damn spriggans won’t know what hit ‘em. Let’s move fast—Ari deserves better than rotting in that cave. Lydia: About time we gut some nature-loving freaks. MJR better be watching when I cleave one in half—nothing gets him hotter than blood on my tits. Valdr: ... I’m just going to focus on killing things quietly now. Lydia: Smartest thing you’ve said all day. Now shut up and bleed quietly if you get stabbed. MJR prefers me when I’m not distracted by whining.
